title: Python Basics
description: Learn Python syntax, variables, collections, conditions, loops, functions, modules, and exception handling.

content.text: Python is a popular programming language. It was created by Guido van Rossum, and released in 1991. It is used for web development (server-side), software development, mathematics, and system scripting.
Python has no command for declaring a variable. A variable is created the moment you first assign a value to it.
Python divides the operators in the following groups: Arithmetic, Assignment, Comparison, Logical, Identity, Membership, and Bitwise.
Python supports the usual logical conditions from mathematics.

content.code:
a = 33
b = 200
if b > a:
  print("b is greater than a")

content.text: Python has two primitive loop commands: while loops and for loops.
Python provides four built-in data types that are used to store collections of data.

items: List: ordered and changeable. Allows duplicate members.
Tuple: ordered and unchangeable. Allows duplicate members.
Set: unordered, unchangeable, and unindexed. No duplicate members.
Dictionary: ordered and changeable. No duplicate members.

content.text: A function is a block of code which only runs when it is called.
Python is an object oriented programming language. Almost everything in Python is an object, with its properties and methods.

content.code:
class MyClass:
  x = 5

p1 = MyClass()
print(p1.x)
